info.title: Hosted Number Management
info.description:
Bandwidth's Hosted Number Management APIs

This API provides endpoints to manage hosted telephone numbers, either for voice and messaging. 

Note that specs are still being moved over from the [Numbers section](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/numbers-apis/numbers)

get.description:
Retrieves the importPhoneNumberOrders requests for the given account ID. A maximum of 1,000 orders can be retrieved per request. If no date range or required query parameter is provided, the order results will be limited to the last two years.

In this table are presented capabilities that are not universally available. The following request query parameters are only allowed for numbers from specific region/country:
| Number Region | Request Query Parameter |
|:--------------|:------------------------|
|NANP|loaType|

post.description:
Creates an importPhoneNumberOrder request to add numbers under the given sub account ID and location ID as specified in the body.
A successfully submitted order will have a status of "RECEIVED". A successfully completed order will have a status of "COMPLETE" if all of the telephone numbers were successfully imported and  "PARTIAL" if some of the telephone numbers were imported. A failed order with will have a staus of "FAILED" and no telephone numbers would have been imported.

The following request parameters are only required for numbers from specific region/country:
| Number Region | Request Parameter |
|:--------------|:------------------------|
|NANP|LoaAuthorizingPerson|
|NANP|LoaType|
|NANP|Subscriber|

get.description: Retrieve an order to import hosted messaging to a phone number by order ID
Retrieves the history of the specified importPhoneNumberOrder.
Retrieves the list of the LOA files associated with the order. Only for orders involving NANP numbers.
get.summary: List Hosted Messaging Order LOA files

post.description:
POSTing to the /loas resource will enable the upload of the file. The key attribute to the POST is ensuring that the headers are correctly set to support the file upload.
Query parameter or header documentType can be used to specify type of document on upload.

When uploading documents using the /loas resource, you must specify the MIME type of the document being upload using the Content-Type HTTP header.

The LOA document must be uploaded using one of the following MIME types:

  • application/pdf
  • text/plain
  • image/jpeg
  • image/png
  • image/tiff
  • text/csv
  • application/vnd.ms-excel
  • application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
  • application/msword
  • application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document
post.summary: Upload LOA File for Import Phone Number Order [NANP]

get.description:
Retrieves the Remove Imported Messaging Tn Orders requests for the given account ID.
A maximum of 1,000 orders can be retrieved per request. If no date range or specific query parameter as required is provided, the order results will be limited to the last two years.
get.summary: List Orders for Removing Hosted Messaging

post.description: Create an order to remove imported phone numbers for messaging from the given account ID. An optional customer ID can be provided in the request body to track the order. A successfully submitted order will have a status of "PROCESSING". A successfully completed order will have a status of "COMPLETE" if all of the phone numbers were successfully removed and  "PARTIAL" if some of the phone numbers were removed. A failed order will have a status of "FAILED" and no phone numbers would have been removed.
post.summary: Create Order to Remove Hosted Messaging

get.description: Retrieve an order to remove hosted messaging from a phone number by ID
get.summary: Get Order to Remove Hosted Messaging

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security 
tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer
of this token.” The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to
protected resources.

- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.

For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management)

HousePrefix.description: The HousePrefix is the non-numeric address number prefix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

HouseNumber.description: The HouseNumber is the street address number of the ServiceAddress. HouseNumber is mandatory for port-in orders in which the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

HouseSuffix.description: The HouseSuffix is the non-numeric address number suffix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

PreDirectional.description: The PreDirectional is the non-numeric street name prefix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

StreetName.description: The StreetName is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

StreetSuffix.description: The StreetSuffix is the street suffix of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

PostDirectional.description: The PostDirectional is the street name post directional of the ServiceAddress. This element is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

AddressLine2.description: AddressLine2 is used to specify Unit, Suite, Floor, etc. in the Service Address. AddressLine2 is optional when not needed to fully specify the ServiceAddress.

City.description: City is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

StateCode.description: StateCode is the 2-letter abbreviation of the state of the ServiceAddress. StateCode is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

Zip.description: Zip is the Zip Code of the ServiceAddress. Zip is mandatory in cases where the ServiceAddress is mandatory.

PlusFour.description: PlusFour is the 4 digits that are sometimes suffixed to the Zip Code.

County.description: The county where the subscriber or business is located.

Country.description: Country is the country of the ServiceAddress. This value will be derived from the StateCode, so it should generally be omitted.

Description.example: The order ID was not found

customerOrderId.description: Internal customer order id for tracking the order. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 255 characters.

phoneNumberCount.description: The count of phone numbers in the order.

orderId.description: The unique identifier string created by Bandwidth to refer to the order.

orderType.description: The type of order. For import phone number orders, this will always be import_tn_orders.

orderDate.description: The date and time the order was created.

accountId.description: The account ID the order is associated with.

userId.description: The ID of the user that created the order.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the order was last modified.

phoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers to be moved

subAccountId.description: A sub account ID to which telephone number will be moved

customerOrderId.description: Internal customer order id for tracking the order. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 40 characters

locationId.description: A location ID of provided site to which telephone number will be moved

loaAuthorizingPerson.description: First and last name of person who authorized LOA.

processingStatus.description:
Status of the Hosted Messaging Order request.

RECEIVED - Order has been received without errors and will be processed through further validation.

PROCESSING - The order has passed initial validation and is now being processed.Order will remain in processing status if any of the requested telphone numbers remain pending processing.

COMPLETE - This implies that this order has been successfully processed and hosted messaging has been enabled on all requested telephone numbers.

PARTIAL - This implies that this order has been successfully processed and hosted messaging was enabled on all except few of the numbers, reasons for same can be found in  Warning section.

FAILED - This implies that this order has been failed. None of the telphone numbers requested were enabled for hosted messaging, reasons for same can be found in Errors and Warning section.

pendingTelephoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers that are pending processing.

failedTelephoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers that failed processing, with reasons detailed in the Warning section.

successTelephoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers successfully processed.

totalCount.description: The total count of orders returned in the response.

ProcessingStatus.description:
Status of the Hosted Messaging Order request.

RECEIVED - Order has been received without errors and will be processed through further validation.

PROCESSING - The order has passed initial validation and is now being processed.Order will remain in processing status if any of the requested telphone numbers remain pending processing.

COMPLETE - This implies that this order has been successfully processed and hosted messaging has been enabled on all requested telephone numbers.

PARTIAL - This implies that this order has been successfully processed and hosted messaging was enabled on all except few of the numbers, reasons for same can be found in  Warning section.

FAILED - This implies that this order has been failed. None of the telphone numbers requested were enabled for hosted messaging, reasons for same can be found in Errors and Warning section.

PendingTelephoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers that are pending processing.

FailedTelephoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers that failed processing, with reasons detailed in the Warning section.

SuccessTelephoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers successfully processed.

TelephoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers to be moved

TelephoneNumber.description: Ten digit phone number with no dots or dashes

SiteId.description: A site id to which telephone number will be moved

CustomerOrderId.description: Internal customer order id for tracking the order. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 40 characters

SipPeerId.description: A sippeer id of provided site to which telephone number will be moved

LoaAuthorizingPerson.description: First and last name of person who authorized LOA.

totalCount.description: The total count of orders returned in the response.

TotalCount.description: The total count of orders returned in the response.

createdDateFromQueryParam.name: createdDateFrom
createdDateFromQueryParam.description: Checks the order's creation date against this value. Orders that have a creation date after this date will be included. Format is yyyy-MM-dd

createdDateToQueryParam.name: createdDateTo
createdDateToQueryParam.description: Checks the order's creation date against this value. Orders that have a creation date before this date will be included. Format is yyyy-MM-dd

customerOrderIdQueryParam.name: customerOrderId
customerOrderIdQueryParam.description: The Customer Order ID is an ID assigned by the account owner to provide a reference number for the order.

documentTypeQueryParam.name: documentType
documentTypeQueryParam.description:
This query parameter allows you to include the document type metadata
at the same time you upload the file.

loaTypeQueryParam.name: loaType
loaTypeQueryParam.description: Indentify the LoaType on phone numbers.

metaDataQueryParam.name: metadata
metaDataQueryParam.description: If true, the response will contain document name and type. If false, the response will only list the file names.

modifiedDateFromQueryParam.name: modifiedDateFrom
modifiedDateFromQueryParam.description: For Date-based searches, the starting date of a date range (inclusive) that will be used to find Import Phone Number Orders that were modified within the date range. It is in the form yyyy-MM-dd.

removeImportedModifiedDateFromQueryParam.name: modifiedDateFrom
removeImportedModifiedDateFromQueryParam.description: For Date-based searches, the starting date of a date range (inclusive) that will be used to find Remove Imported Phone Number Orders that were modified within the date range. It is in the form yyyy-MM-dd.

modifiedDateToQueryParam.name: modifiedDateTo
modifiedDateToQueryParam.description: For Date-based searches, the ending date of a date range (inclusive) that will be used to find Import Phone Number Orders that were modified within the date range. It is in the form yyyy-MM-dd.

removeImportedModifiedDateToQueryParam.name: modifiedDateTo
removeImportedModifiedDateToQueryParam.description: For Date-based searches, the ending date of a date range (inclusive) that will be used to find Remove Imported Phone Number Orders that were modified within the date range. It is in the form yyyy-MM-dd.

phoneNumberQueryParam.name: phoneNumber
phoneNumberQueryParam.description: A phone number that is referenced in the order

statusQueryParam.name: status
statusQueryParam.description: The status of the order being searched for.

example.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<OrderHistoryWrapper>
   <OrderHistory>
     <OrderDate>2015-06-16T14:03:10.225Z</OrderDate>
     <Note>Import TN order is received.</Note>
     <Author>admin</Author>
     <Status>RECEIVED</Status>
   </OrderHistory>
   <OrderHistory>
     <OrderDate>2015-06-16T14:03:10.330Z</OrderDate>
     <Note>Import TN order is processing.</Note>
     <Author>admin</Author>
     <Status>PROCESSING</Status>
   </OrderHistory>
   <OrderHistory>
     <OrderDate>2015-06-16T14:03:10.789Z</OrderDate>
     <Note>Import TN order is partial.</Note>
     <Author>admin</Author>
     <Status>PARTIAL</Status>
   </OrderHistory>
 </OrderHistoryWrapper>

getRemoveImportedMessagingPhoneNumberOrd.description: The descriptive payload for the Remove Imported Messaging Tn Orders query provides information about the orders found by the query, including the data associated with the order, the state of the order, and a list of the successfully removed Telephone Numbers, and descriptions of any encountered errors.
